en.extract:
The white-eared night heron (Oroanassa magnifica) is a species of heron in the family Ardeidae. It was once thought to be only found in southern China and in northern Vietnam. However, its range is actually significantly larger as in the 2010s and 2020s has been recently discovered in Cambodia, India and Bangladesh.
In 2017, it was camera-trapped in the Cardamom Mountains. In 2018, it was also reported in Valmiki National Park, India and in 2025, it was once again camera-trapped in Namdapha National Park, India. It was discovered in the Sundarbans in Bangladesh in 2022. It is threatened by habitat loss and habitat fragmentation.


Taxonomy
The white-eared night heron was collected in Hainan by John Whitehead. It was described as Nycticorax magnifica by William Robert Ogilvie-Grant in 1899. The species is monotypic.


Description
The length is 54–56 cm (21–22 in). The male is mostly blackish-brown. The neck-sides are chestnut. The lores are yellow, and the beak is black. The eyes are yellow-orange. The head and nape are blackish. The postocular stripes and throat are white. The underparts are brown, with white streaks. The tarsi are green. The female is similar to the male, but its head and neck are less distinctly patterned. The female also has whitish streaks on its back and wings. The juvenile is similar to the female, but has a browner plumage and buff spots.


Distribution and habitat
This heron is found in southern China, India and northern Vietnam. Its range size is estimated at 2,180,000 km2 (840,000 sq mi). By 2001, the species had only been recorded from about 20 localities; in the ten years from 2001 to 2011, surveys discovered it in more than 30 localities, making its known range much larger. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical forests and rivers. It has also been found in human-modified habitats.


Behaviour and ecology
Like other night herons, this species is mostly nocturnal. It feeds on fish, shrimps and invertebrates. Its territorial call is a deep, raspy whoaa, lasting about 0.3 seconds and repeated every 5–15 seconds. Breeding has been recorded in both Vietnam and China. The clutch size is 3–5 eggs. In China, hatching has been observed in May, with an incubation period of about 25 days. In Vietnam, fledging has been observed in late April. Breeding seems to occur earlier in Vietnam than in China. The nest is in the shape of a circular tray. A study found that fledging occurred more than two months after hatching, longer than most other species of heron.


Status and conservation
The IUCN Red List listed the white-eared night heron as an endangered species from 2000 – 2025, following its downlisting from Critically Endangered in 2000. These classifications were due to a small estimated range and population. The species has become much better studied in the 21st century, with new records across India, Cambodia, and Bangladesh; its population (1,500 – 15,000 mature individuals) and range are now estimated to be considerably larger than previously thought. Because of this, the white-eared night heron was downlisted to Near Threatened in 2025.
It is threatened by deforestation, hunting, overfishing and water pollution. It has been discovered in many new localities, but the population is probably declining because of the threats. The species is listed as a Class II protected species in China. It occurs in protected areas in China and Vietnam, such as the Chebaling National Nature Reserve and Ba Be National Park.


References


External links
 Media related to white-eared night heron at Wikimedia Commons
 Data related to white-eared night heron at Wikispecies

The '''white-eared night heron''' ('''''Oroanassa magnifica''''') is a species of [[heron]] in the family Ardeidae. It was once thought to be only found in southern [[China]] and in northern [[Vietnam]]. However, its range is actually significantly larger as in the 2010s and 2020s has been recently discovered in [[Cambodia]], [[India]] and [[Bangladesh|Bangladesh.]]

In 2017, it was [[Camera trap|camera-trapped]] in the [[Cardamom Mountains]]. In 2018, it was also reported in [[Valmiki National Park]], [[India]]<ref>{{cite journal|last1=SHAFI|first1=SHARIQ|title= 'LITTLE KNOWN ASIAN SPECIES Extraordinary discovery in Bihar, India: an apparent White-eared Night Heron Gorsachius magnificus in Valmiki Tiger Reserve'|journal=Birdingasia |date=2018|issue=29|pages=9–10}}</ref> and in 2025, it was once again [[Camera trap|camera-trapped]] in [[Namdapha National Park]], India.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Karmakar |first=Sumir |date=2 July 2025 |title=Elusive white-eared night heron camera trapped in Namdapha park, second sighting in India |url=https://www.deccanherald.com/india/arunachal-pradesh/elusive-white-eared-night-heron-camera-trapped-in-namdapha-park-second-sighting-in-india-3613110 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250728234952/https://www.deccanherald.com/india/arunachal-pradesh/elusive-white-eared-night-heron-camera-trapped-in-namdapha-park-second-sighting-in-india-3613110 |archive-date=28 July 2025 |access-date=10 August 2025 |website=Deccan Herald}}</ref> It was discovered in the [[Sundarbans]] in [[Bangladesh]] in 2022.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Li |first1=Binqiang |last2=Ma |first2=Shanjun |last3=Bibi |first3=Nehafta |last4=Yang |first4=Miaodan |last5=Tan |first5=Lifeng |date=2025-06-12 |title=New distribution sites and local population trends of the white-eared night heron (Gorsachius magnificus) in China |journal=Biodiversity Data Journal |language=en |volume=13 |article-number=e159088 |doi=10.3897/BDJ.13.e159088 |doi-access=free |pmid=40547936 |pmc=12179649 |issn=1314-2828}}</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]] and [[habitat fragmentation]].<ref name="birdlife">{{cite web|title=Species factsheet: ''Gorsachius magnificus''|url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/white-eared-night-heron-gorsachius-magnificus/text|website=birdlife.org|publisher=[[BirdLife International]]|date=2016|access-date=8 October 2016}}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
The white-eared night heron was collected in [[Hainan]] by [[John Whitehead (explorer)|John Whitehead]]. It was described as ''Nycticorax magnifica'' by [[William Robert Ogilvie-Grant]] in 1899.<ref>{{cite journal|last1=Ogilvie-Grant|first1=W. R.|title=Descriptions of seven new Species of Birds from the Interior of Hainan|journal=Ibis|date=1899|volume=5|issue=4|pages=584–587|doi=10.1111/j.1474-919x.1899.tb05570.x|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/9036127|series=7}}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]].<ref name=Brazil />

==Description==
The length is {{convert|54|-|56|cm|abbr=on}}.<ref name=birdlife /> The male is mostly blackish-brown. The neck-sides are chestnut.<ref name=Brazil>{{cite book|last1=Brazil|first1=Mark|title=Birds of East Asia|date=2009|publisher=A&C Black|isbn=978-0-7136-7040-0|page=100|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7-GgBoivL70C&pg=PA100}}</ref> The [[lore (anatomy)|lores]] are yellow, and the beak is black. The eyes are yellow-orange.<ref name=Brazil /> The head and [[nape]] are blackish. The postocular stripes and throat are white. The underparts are brown, with white streaks.<ref name=birdlife /> The tarsi are green.<ref name=Brazil /> The female is similar to the male, but its head and neck are less distinctly patterned. The female also has whitish streaks on its back and wings.<ref name=birdlife /> The [[juvenile (organism)|juvenile]] is similar to the female, but has a browner plumage and buff spots.<ref name=birdlife />

==Distribution and habitat==
This heron is found in southern China, India and northern Vietnam. Its range size is estimated at {{convert|2180000|km2|abbr=on}}.<ref name=IUCN /> By 2001, the species had only been recorded from about 20 localities; in the ten years from 2001 to 2011, surveys discovered it in more than 30 localities, making its known range much larger.<ref name=birdlife /><ref name=He>{{cite journal|last1=He|first1=Fenqi|last2=Yang|first2=Xiaojun|last3=Deng|first3=Xuejian|last4=Zhu|first4=Kaiming|last5=Li|first5=Li|last6=Lin|first6=Jiansheng|last7=Jiang|first7=Handong|last8=Zhi|first8=Lin|title=The White-eared Night Heron (''Gorsachius magnificus''): from behind the bamboo curtain to the front stage|journal=Chinese Birds|date=2011|volume=2|issue=4|pages=163–166|doi=10.5122/cbirds.2011.0025|doi-access=free}}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical [[forest]]s and [[river]]s. It has also been found in human-modified habitats.<ref name=birdlife />

==Behaviour and ecology==
Like other [[night heron]]s, this species is mostly [[nocturnal]]. It feeds on fish, shrimps and invertebrates.<ref name=birdlife /> Its territorial call is a deep, raspy ''whoaa'', lasting about 0.3 seconds and repeated every 5–15 seconds.<ref>{{cite journal|last1=King|first1=Ben|title=Vocalisation of the White-eared Night Heron ''Gorsachius magnificus''|journal=Forktail|date=2005|issue=21|pages=177–178|url=http://orientalbirdclub.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/09/King-NightHeron.pdf|publisher=Oriental Bird Club}}</ref> Breeding has been recorded in both Vietnam and China. The clutch size is 3–5 eggs.<ref name=Pilgrim /> In China, hatching has been observed in May, with an incubation period of about 25 days. In Vietnam, [[fledging]] has been observed in late April. Breeding seems to occur earlier in Vietnam than in China.<ref name=Pilgrim>{{cite journal|last1=Pilgrim|first1=John D.|last2=Walsh|first2=David F.|last3=Thanh Tu|first3=Tran|last4=Duc Tu|first4=Nguyen|last5=Eames|first5=Jonathan C.|last6=Manh Hung|first6=Le|title=The Endangered White-eared Night Heron ''Gorsachius magnificus'' in Vietnam: status, distribution, ecology and threats|journal=Forktail|date=2009|issue=25|pages=142–146|url=http://orientalbirdclub.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/10/Pilgrim-White-earedNight-Heron.pdf|publisher=Oriental Bird Club}}</ref> The nest is in the shape of a circular tray. A study found that fledging occurred more than two months after hatching, longer than most other species of heron.<ref name=Gao>{{cite journal|last1=Gao|first1=Jianyun|last2=Wu|first2=Zhaolu|last3=Su|first3=Dongdong|last4=Zhong|first4=Pingsheng|last5=Peng|first5=Yisheng|last6=Zou|first6=Fasheng|title=Observations on breeding behavior of the White-eared Night Heron (''Gorsachius magnificus'') in northern Guangdong, China|journal=Chinese Birds|date=2013|volume=4|issue=3|pages=254–259|doi=10.5122/cbirds.2013.0024|doi-access=free |bibcode=2013AvRes...4..254G }}</ref>

==Status and conservation==
The [[IUCN Red List]] listed the white-eared night heron as an [[endangered species]] from 2000 – 2025, following its downlisting from Critically Endangered in 2000. These classifications were due to a small estimated range and population.<ref name=IUCN /><ref name="He" /> The species has become much better studied in the 21st century, with new records across India, Cambodia, and Bangladesh; its population (1,500 – 15,000 mature individuals) and range are now estimated to be considerably larger than previously thought. Because of this, the white-eared night heron was downlisted to Near Threatened in 2025.<ref name="IUCN" />

It is threatened by deforestation, hunting, overfishing and water pollution.<ref name="birdlife" /> It has been discovered in many new localities, but the population is probably declining because of the threats.<ref name="birdlife" /> The species is listed as a Class II protected species in China.<ref name="Gao" /> It occurs in protected areas in China and Vietnam, such as the {{Interlanguage link multi|Chebaling National Nature Reserve|zh|3=车八岭}} and [[Ba Be National Park]].<ref name="birdlife" />

zh.extract:
海南鳽（學名：Oroanassa magnifica），又名海南虎斑鳽、海南麻鷺，为鹭科海南𫛚属（學名：Oroanassa）的唯一物種，有時亦有「鳥中大熊貓」的稱號。分布于中国海南、广西、安徽、福建、浙江等地和越南，一般生活于山间河谷及附近水域。该物种的模式产地在海南五指山。
其种加词“Magnificus ”意为“极好的”、“华丽的”。


保护
中国国家重点保护动物等级：一级
